title: Incorrect Stack Overflow Handling: Misbalanced Elements

statement.markdown: Design a stack implementation to validate parentheses for an arithmetic expression. The stack should handle overflow conditions appropriately, and it should return True if the input string has balanced parentheses.
statement.inputFormat: A string containing parentheses and operators.
statement.outputFormat: True if the expression has balanced parentheses, False otherwise.
statement.notes: You can use any additional data structures you like, but implement the stack from scratch.

hints.text: Use a custom stack implementation to handle overflow conditions.
Recall the basic principles of stack-based expression parsing.

editorial.title: Invalid Stack-Based Expression Evaluator
editorial.intuition: The goal is to identify whether the input expression has balanced parentheses.
editorial.bruteForce: Implementing a simple stack-based approach using a built-in stack data structure would suffice.
editorial.betterSolution: Implementing a custom stack from scratch would help handle potential overflow conditions correctly.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a recursive function to validate the input string directly, avoiding the need for a stack altogether.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of our approach can be verified by checking the base cases and the recursive function.
editorial.dryRun: An example dry run of the optimal solution to understand its behavior.
commonMistakes: Overlooked the potential for overflow conditions when using a built-in stack.
Failed to correctly handle nested parentheses in the input string.

functionSignature.cpp: class Stack { public: ...; } void validate(string s);
functionSignature.java: class Stack { public ...; } public boolean validate(String s);
